01
THE AGENT IS THE PRODUCT
Wave Vision

"Wave's 150,000 agents aren't a distribution channel — they are the product. The moment their experience becomes seamless, client trust and growth follow as a natural consequence."

Across Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire, and Uganda, most users' experience of Wave is mediated through an agent — not the app. An agent who struggles to onboard, gets confused by a new feature, or loses a transaction doesn't just lose that transaction. They erode the client's trust in Wave entirely.

I've lived this problem directly. At One Acre Fund, our shop agents had a 4.7/10 trust score in our POS system. Transactions failed daily. The real issue was simple: we had never understood what the agent's day actually looked like. When we fixed that, everything else improved. That is exactly where I'd start at Wave.

01
Deep Agent Discovery

Run structured user discovery across Wave's key markets. Map the full agent workflow end-to-end: onboarding friction, feature adoption gaps, transaction failure points, and the exact moments where fraud finds its opening. Not a survey — observation, interviews, ride-alongs.

02
Frictionless Onboarding & Feature Rollout

New agents and new features should feel effortless to absorb. Define an onboarding flow and in-app training that gets agents productive fast — closing the window of uncertainty where mistakes and fraud are most likely.

03
Close Fraud Gaps at the Workflow Level

Fraud happens in the gaps between steps — when an agent is rushed or outside their normal flow. Map those friction points and reduce the opportunity surface through smarter UX, not just back-end detection.

04
Measure Trust — Not Just Transactions

Define an agent satisfaction and client trust metric feeding directly into expansion decisions. If clients love their agent, growth into new markets becomes a natural consequence of the foundation you've built.

The Outcome

Agents who are confident, well-equipped, and operating with minimal friction become Wave's most powerful growth engine. Client trust in Wave is built one agent interaction at a time. Get that right, and expansion into Cameroon, new demographics, savings and credit products becomes a natural byproduct.

02
I DON'T WAIT FOR ENGINEERS
Live Work

"The fastest way to get feedback on a product idea isn't a PRD — it's a working prototype you can put in someone's hands today."

When I want to test a product direction, I build it myself. I write the code, deploy it to GitHub Pages, and share a live link for immediate feedback from real users and stakeholders.

This is how I work: product instinct + technical execution + fast feedback loop. It's how I shipped Tupande Online in 8 months, and how I tested the Acre Point POS UX before a single engineer wrote production code.

Wave PMs work without a speed limit. This is what that looks like in practice.

03
BUILT FOR HOW WAVE WORKS
Why Me
Wave looks for
PMs who lead like founders. Set own goals. Own the direction.
Doreen's proof

I pioneered One Acre Fund's PRD framework from scratch — no template, no playbook. When our POS was failing agents, I built the executive business case, ran a 197-person survey, and drove the engineering warroom. I don't wait to be pointed at problems.

Wave looks for
Optimize for learning. Ship MVPs. Maximize learning per unit of effort.
Doreen's proof

Launched Tupande Online in 8 months — started lean, measured constantly, iterated fast. Hit 30K users and $70K revenue. When order completion was at 23%, I isolated the riskiest drop-off points first. It hit 96%.

Wave looks for
Overreach. Act like a mini-GM. Do whatever the outcome requires.
Doreen's proof

At IBM I ran operations across 20+ African markets. At One Acre Fund, when Odoo degradation was crippling agents pre-harvest, I didn't file a ticket — I built an executive escalation case and pushed for a dedicated warroom. I find another path.

Wave looks for
Meet users where they are. Travel. Observe daily routines firsthand.
Doreen's proof

My users are smallholder farmers in rural Kenya — feature phones, no prior fintech experience, intermittent connectivity. I do field visits regularly. The Acre Point UX was shaped by watching agents handle M-Pesa transactions under peak-season pressure — not from a boardroom.

04
OWNED END-TO-END
Products
01
E-commerce · Fintech
Tupande Online
Lead PM — Full Product Lifecycle

Farmers in rural Kenya needed to order inputs digitally — without bank accounts, reliable data, or prior e-commerce experience. I defined the problem from first principles, wrote the specs, drove UX decisions, and shipped in 8 months.

  • 30,000 active users acquired
  • $70,000 revenue in first 8 months
  • Order completion 23% → 96%
  • Won Top Agriculture App award
02
POS · Payments · Fraud
Acre Point POS
Lead PM — Research through Prototyping

Agent trust score was 4.7/10. Transaction failures were daily. I surveyed 197 users, identified the failure modes in their actual workflows, and defined a new agent-optimised POS with M-Pesa integration and in-session fraud detection.

  • 197-person field survey uncovered trust crisis
  • Fraud prevention on $200M+ in payment flows
  • Full product epics with detailed acceptance criteria
  • Executive dashboard tracking deployment readiness
03
Credit · Identity · Fintech
Loan Eligibility Engine
Lead PM — Workflow Design & Outcomes

Designing credit eligibility for borrowers with no formal credit history requires deep understanding of how farmers manage money seasonally. I built the eligibility logic from scratch — balancing inclusion with repayment viability across 10 countries.

  • 90% loan repayment rate achieved
  • Built on Mifos/Fineract + custom identity flows
  • Keycloak & OAuth for secure access
  • Scaled across 10 countries
04
Platform · ERP · Operations
Retail Operations Platform
Lead PM — ERP & Supply Chain

Managing inventory and operations across hundreds of shops requires knowing when the system is breaking before agents do. I managed the Odoo ERP, payment integrations, and built dashboards surfacing systemic failures in real time.

  • Managed hundreds of retail locations across Kenya
  • Analysed 10,000+ Jira tickets to surface patterns
  • Built roadmaps with T-shirt sizing 2026–2028
  • 94% inventory migration match rate
05
I GO WHERE THE USERS ARE
Field Work

Wave builds for people across Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire, and Uganda — feature phones, inconsistent connectivity, markets where a failed transaction ruins someone's day. I understand that world. My users have lived it.

"If the data doesn't make sense, my first instinct is to get in front of the user — not run another query."

🇰🇪 Rural Kenya — Shop Agent Visits
Watching transactions fail under real pressure

Spent days at Tupande retail shops watching agents process M-Pesa transactions during peak harvest season. Saw exactly where the POS failed — timeout errors, unconfirmed payment states, confusion on retry flows. Built the new UX from those observations, not assumptions.

🌾 Farmer Interviews — Input Ordering
How farmers actually think about digital money

Many of our farmers had never used e-commerce. I interviewed them directly about trust, payment anxiety, and what "confirmation" meant to them. This reshaped our order confirmation UX — SMS fallbacks, simplified receipts — all from what I heard in the field.

📊 197-Person Survey — POS Reliability
Quantifying a trust crisis no one had named

When agents complained, leadership assumed minor friction. I ran a structured 197-person survey across Kenya and found a 4.7/10 trust score and widespread failures. That data became the business case for a full rebuild and an engineering warroom before peak season.
